events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess a residential panelboard installation. The observed condition: "double-tapped breakers (non-listed)". This means a circuit breaker terminal has two conductors connected, but the breaker is not listed for two conductors. This is a code violation. NEC 110.14(A) requires terminals to be used according to their listing. Most standard breakers are only listed for one conductor unless specifically marked for two. So this is non-compliant. The work stage is rough-complete. So decision: fail. Risk: medium? Double-tapping can cause loose connections, overheating, arcing, fire hazard. So risk is high or critical? I'd say high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 80%? Panelboard installed but not closed out. Value: not applicable. Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: "Double-tapped breaker (non-listed) violates NEC 110.14(A) and manufacturer listing." Actions: "Remove extra conductor and install a separate breaker or use a listed tandem breaker if panel allows." Rationale: "Breakers not listed for two conductors create a fire hazard." References: NEC 110.14(A), 110.3(B) (listing), maybe 408.41? Actually 408.41 is about grounded conductor, not relevant. 200.4 is about neutral conductors, not relevant. So I'll cite 110.14(A) and 110.3(B). Also manufacturer instructions. So output JSON.

events.label: response

    "Double-tapped breaker (non-listed) – two conductors under one terminal not rated for multiple wires."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove extra conductor and install a separate breaker or use a listed tandem breaker if panel allows.",
    "Verify all terminations comply with manufacturer listing and NEC 110.14(A)."
  ],
  "rationale": "Breakers not listed for two conductors create a loose connection, overheating, and fire hazard. NEC 110.14(A) requires terminals to be used per their listing, and 110.3(B) requires equipment to be installed per listing instructions.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14(A)",
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "Manufacturer listing instructions"
  ]
}
